content:
Post 1

ASX 200 fell 20.10 points or 0.23% to 8,633.20, slipping below its 20-day moving average. The index has shed 0.61% over five sessions. A tech-led selloff drove the weakness, with IT stocks absorbing the heaviest selling pressure across the board.

Post 2

Information Technology dropped 2.24%, the session's worst sector. Energy led gains at +1.46%. AAI was the standout loser, falling 8.34% to AUD 93.90 — Alcoa's sharp decline dragged on materials sentiment. TUA topped the gainers, adding 5.88% to AUD 2.70.

Post 3

Gold is sitting at AUD 5,872.49 per oz with AUD/USD at 0.6994. A break below 0.6950 would amplify import cost pressures and weigh on rate-sensitive financials, which already lost 1.45% Thursday. Watch whether the AUD holds this level as US sessions develop overnight.

Post 1

ASX 200 closed at 8,633.20, down 20.10 points or 0.23%, slipping below its 20-day moving average. The index has shed 0.61% over five sessions. Tech selling drove the weakness, with IT the clear drag as risk appetite rotated away from growth names.

Post 2

IT fell 2.24% to lead declines; Energy gained 1.46% to lead advances. NEXTDC (NXT) dropped 4.23% to AUD 14.50 as the tech selloff hit data centre names hard. On the other side, Karoon Energy (KAR) rose 4.59% to AUD 2.05, riding the energy sector bid.

Post 3

Gold at AUD 5,857.39/oz is the level to watch. With the ASX flat year-to-date and IT under pressure, any further USD strength — the AUD sits at 0.6989 — could push gold higher in local terms and test whether materials can sustain their modest 0.29% gain into next week.
